items.text:
Subject: Introducing the Campus Tool Library

Dear Riverside Residents,

We're excited to announce that the Campus Tool Library has opened in the basement of Hartwell Hall, Room B12. Any student with a valid ID can now borrow hand tools, power drills, garden equipment, and sewing machines instead of buying them. Borrowing is free for standard items, though a $5 deposit is required for power tools and is fully returned when you bring them back undamaged.

The library is open Monday through Friday, 1:00 PM to 6:00 PM. Each item may be kept for up to seven days. To borrow anything, please register once at the front desk and read the short safety guide for power equipment. Items returned late are charged a $2 fee per day.

We also welcome donations of gently used tools. If you have something to share, drop it off during open hours and our staff will check it before adding it to the collection.

Thanks for helping build a more sustainable campus.

Best regards,
Dana Okafor
Resource Center Coordinator

questions.stem: According to the email, what is required before a student can borrow a power tool?

options.A: A signed donation form from the front desk
options.B: A refundable $5 deposit and one-time registration
options.C: A faculty approval submitted to the coordinator
options.D: A seven-day reservation made online in advance

questions.explanation: The email states a '$5 deposit is required for power tools' and that borrowers must 'register once at the front desk,' so both conditions apply to power tools.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To inform students about a new tool-borrowing service
options.B: To remind students about overdue tool return fees
options.C: To request donations of used garden equipment
options.D: To announce changes to Hartwell Hall opening hours

questions.explanation: The email's central aim is to announce the opening of the Campus Tool Library and explain how students can use it; fees and donations are supporting details, not the main point.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a student who returns a power tool undamaged and on time?

options.A: They will be added to a list of approved donors
options.B: They will receive their deposit back in full
options.C: They will be charged a $2 daily handling fee
options.D: They will be allowed to keep the tool longer

questions.explanation: The email says the $5 deposit 'is fully returned when you bring them back undamaged,' and the $2 fee applies only to late returns, so an on-time, undamaged return means the full deposit is returned.

items.text:
NOTICE: End-of-Semester Textbook Buyback

The annual textbook buyback will be held during finals week. Because the Student Union lobby is under renovation, the event has been moved to the Carver Library Atrium. Students who wish to sell used textbooks should note the updated details below.

Date: Monday, May 18 through Wednesday, May 20
Time: 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM
Location: Carver Library Atrium (first floor)

The following guidelines must be observed:

- A current student ID must be presented at the buyback table.
- Only books in resellable condition will be accepted; titles with water damage or missing pages will be declined.
- Payment is issued in cash, with most titles valued between $8 and $40.
- A maximum of six books per student may be sold each day.

Prices are determined by the publisher's current edition list and cannot be negotiated. Books for courses being discontinued next year may not be purchased. Faculty desk copies are not eligible for buyback.

Questions should be directed to the Course Materials Office at (555) 204-8819 or coursematerials@riverside.edu.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is the maximum number of books a student may sell in a single day?

options.A: A total of three books per visit
options.B: A total of four books per visit
options.C: A total of five books per visit
options.D: A total of six books per visit

questions.explanation: The notice states 'A maximum of six books per student may be sold each day.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about why the buyback location was changed?

options.A: The library offered more cash for textbooks
options.B: The usual lobby was unavailable due to repairs
options.C: The event attracted too many students last year
options.D: The finals schedule required an earlier start time

questions.explanation: The notice explains the event moved 'Because the Student Union lobby is under renovation,' implying the usual space was unavailable for repairs.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To explain how textbook prices are set by publishers
options.B: To encourage students to donate unwanted course books
options.C: To inform students about the buyback's rules and new location
options.D: To announce the renovation of the Student Union lobby

questions.explanation: The notice's overall purpose is to tell students about the textbook buyback, including its relocated venue and the rules for selling; the pricing, donations, and renovation are only supporting details.
